exercises.justification_from_packet: Theory/demo norm_doc and norm_name with casefold.

exercises.code:
def blocking_key(rec):
    toks = rec['name'].casefold().split()
    ap = toks[1] if len(toks) >= 2 else toks[0]
    return f"{ap}|{rec['region'].casefold()}"
print(blocking_key({"name": "Luis Huamán Soto", "region": "Cusco"}))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Hints: paternal surname second token + region; format ap|region.
Instruction 1.0/0.5/0.0 rules from packet.

print("recall", round(tp / (tp + fn), 3))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Theory TP/FP/FN precision recall.

exercises.code:
pairs = [
    {"id": "P1", "score": 0.2},
    {"id": "P2", "score": 0.55},
    {"id": "P3", "score": 0.7},
    {"id": "P4", "score": 0.9},
]
def clerical_queue(pairs, low=0.4, high=0.7):
    return [p['id'] for p in pairs if low <= p['score'] <= high]
print(clerical_queue(pairs))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Theory doubt band → clerical queue.

exercises.code:
def explanation_bullets(er, rel, missing):
    return [
        f"entity_resolution_score={er}",
        f"relationship_signal_score={rel}",
        f"missing={missing}",
    ]
print(explanation_bullets(0.9, 0.4, ["phone"]))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Theory legible bullets with ER, rel, missing.
Hints order conflict first; missing length rules.

exercises.code:
er, rel = 0.9, 0.1
score = round(0.6 * er + 0.4 * rel, 3)
conflict = abs(er - rel) > 0.5
print("evidence_score", score)
print("uncertainty", "high" if conflict else "low")
print("note", "señales conflictivas")
exercises.justification_from_packet: Starter/instruction: do not polish score when signals conflict.
